species: Ammonia-oxidizing archaea (AOA), dominated by 'Ca. Nitrosotenuis' / Nitrosopumilaceae (community, not a defined isolate)

growth_records.medium: soil slurry microcosm, 0x MWM (mineral water medium, no added basic salts; EC ~60 uS/cm)

up.method: nitrate production monitored; per-biomass ammonia oxidation rate not reported

sec.method: colorimetric nitrate measurement over incubation; used to derive AOA specific growth rate

growth_records.cite: Gwak, Joo-Han et al. Hypoosmolarity inhibits ammonia oxidation by terrestrial and freshwater Nitrosopumilaceae members. The ISME Journal (2026). DOI:10.1093/ismejo/wrag045. [PMC13037477]
growth_records.snippet: AOA-specific growth rates, calculated based on nitrate production, were lower under 0x and 1x MWM conditions (0.015 d-1 and 0.020 d-1, respectively, in both soils) compared to 10x MWM conditions (0.027 d-1 and 0.032 d-1 in grassland and agr
growth_records.notes: Reported mu = 0.015 d-1 (0x MWM, EC ~60 uS/cm, both grassland and agricultural soils). Converted to per-h: 0.015/24 = 0.000625 h-1. Doubling time = ln2/mu = 0.693/0.015 = 46.2 d = 1108.8 h. This is a soil-microcosm COMMUNITY AOA-specific growth rate (qPCR/nitrate-derived), NOT a defined pure-culture physiological rate; carbon fixation (chemolithoautotroph). Substrate = 100 uM ammonia. Temperature 25 C, dark. No biomass-normalized uptake/secretion rates given.

growth_records.medium: soil slurry microcosm, 1x MWM (mineral water medium; EC ~120 uS/cm)
growth_records.cite: Gwak, Joo-Han et al. Hypoosmolarity inhibits ammonia oxidation by terrestrial and freshwater Nitrosopumilaceae members. The ISME Journal (2026). DOI:10.1093/ismejo/wrag045. [PMC13037477]
growth_records.snippet: AOA-specific growth rates, calculated based on nitrate production, were lower under 0x and 1x MWM conditions (0.015 d-1 and 0.020 d-1, respectively, in both soils) compared to 10x MWM conditions (0.027 d-1 and 0.032 d-1 in grassland and agr
growth_records.notes: Reported mu = 0.020 d-1 (1x MWM, EC ~120 uS/cm, both soils). Per-h: 0.020/24 = 0.000833 h-1. Doubling = 0.693/0.020 = 34.65 d = 831.6 h. Community AOA soil-microcosm rate; not a pure-culture rate. Substrate 100 uM ammonia, 25 C, dark, aerobic.

growth_records.medium: soil slurry microcosm, 10x MWM (mineral water medium; EC ~780 uS/cm)
growth_records.cite: Gwak, Joo-Han et al. Hypoosmolarity inhibits ammonia oxidation by terrestrial and freshwater Nitrosopumilaceae members. The ISME Journal (2026). DOI:10.1093/ismejo/wrag045. [PMC13037477]
growth_records.snippet: compared to 10x MWM conditions (0.027 d-1 and 0.032 d-1 in grassland and agricultural soils, respectively), indicating that AOA growth is sensitive to lower salinity
growth_records.notes: Reported mu = 0.027 d-1 (grassland soil, 10x MWM control, EC ~780 uS/cm). Per-h: 0.027/24 = 0.001125 h-1. Doubling = 0.693/0.027 = 25.67 d = 616 h. Community AOA soil-microcosm rate; 100 uM ammonia, 25 C, dark, aerobic. This 10x condition is the high-salinity control that gave the fastest AOA growth.

growth_records.medium: soil slurry microcosm, 10x MWM (mineral water medium; EC ~780 uS/cm)
growth_records.cite: Gwak, Joo-Han et al. Hypoosmolarity inhibits ammonia oxidation by terrestrial and freshwater Nitrosopumilaceae members. The ISME Journal (2026). DOI:10.1093/ismejo/wrag045. [PMC13037477]
growth_records.snippet: compared to 10x MWM conditions (0.027 d-1 and 0.032 d-1 in grassland and agricultural soils, respectively), indicating that AOA growth is sensitive to lower salinity
growth_records.notes: Reported mu = 0.032 d-1 (agricultural soil, 10x MWM control, EC ~780 uS/cm). Per-h: 0.032/24 = 0.001333 h-1. Doubling = 0.693/0.032 = 21.66 d = 519.8 h. Community AOA soil-microcosm rate; 100 uM ammonia, 25 C, dark, aerobic. Note: pure-culture isolates (N. koreense MY1, Ca. N. chungbukensis MY2, N. viennensis EN76, Ca. N. oleophilus MY3, Ca. N. inopinata ENR4, N. europaea ATCC 19718) were also grown batch in MWM, but the main text gives only RELATIVE growth retention (e.g. 7.9%, 22.5%, 81.9%, 80.7% of 10x-MWM rate), no absolute mu -> not separately recorded.
